objective: The scalar SHA-256 profile is best addressed in current OpenSSL by documenting and validating the runtime RISC-V capability selection path: optimized SHA-256 implementations are present, but benchmarks must confirm hwprobe or OPENSSL_riscvcap exposes Zbb/vector crypto capabilities instead of assuming -march alone controls dispatch.

changes.rationale: The scalar SHA-256 profile is best addressed in current OpenSSL by documenting and validating the runtime RISC-V capability selection path: optimized SHA-256 implementations are present, but benchmarks must confirm hwprobe or OPENSSL_riscvcap exposes Zbb/vector crypto capabilities instead of assuming -march alone controls dispatch.
instructions: In the examples section, add a RISC-V SHA-256 benchmarking example that enables ZBB through OPENSSL_riscvcap.
Add a second example for the vector SHA-256 path that includes V, ZVKB, and one SHA-2 vector crypto extension.
State that these examples are for testing or benchmarking override scenarios and that normal production use should prefer kernel hwprobe detection.
Avoid changing capability parsing semantics or generic RISC-V build targets.

expectedDiffSummary: Document SHA-256-specific OPENSSL_riscvcap examples so RISC-V benchmarks can force and verify the Zbb or vector crypto dispatch paths without changing generic RISC-V build targets.
